title: "Upon The Gallows Hung A Wretch,"
content:
Upon the gallows hung a wretch,
    Too sullied for the hell
    To which the law entitled him.
    As nature's curtain fell
    The one who bore him tottered in,
    For this was woman's son.
    ''T was all I had,' she stricken gasped;
    Oh, what a livid boon!
meaning:
""Upon The Gallows Hung A Wretch,"" is a quintessential example of Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
